last_completed_round.direction_name: Revisit the 128x128x32 hot-band branch on top of the current grouped-order plus launch-bounds base
last_completed_round.hypothesis: Earlier 128x128x32 attempts were measured before the grouped-order and compiler-clue improvements that define the current best branch. Those changes materially altered the residency regime: the current winner already runs in a 2-CTA register-limited mode, which narrows the occupancy disadvantage that previously hurt K32. That makes it worth re-testing the 128x128x32 branch with the same grouped CTA order and the same mild `launch_bounds(128, 2)` guidance, because K32 still offers the best chance to reduce mainloop control overhead if its old occupancy penalty is no longer the dominant cost.

notes: Build passed for round 30/50. Node A will measure the result next.
